title: Podman machine can't find win-sshproxy
body:
### Issue Description

I've updated my windows podman installation using `scoop`. 

Now, when I start the `podman machine`, I get the folling error: 

```
PS C:\Users\ruben> podman machine start
Starting machine "podman-machine-default"

This machine is currently configured in rootless mode. If your containers
require root permissions (e.g. ports < 1024), or if you run into compatibility
issues with non-podman clients, you can switch using the following command:

        podman machine set --rootful

time="2025-12-12T12:02:31-08:00" level=warning msg="Failed to find $BINDIR: The system cannot find the path specified."
API forwarding for Docker API clients is not available due to the following startup failures.
        could not find "win-sshproxy.exe" in one of [$BINDIR].  To resolve this error, set the helper_binaries_dir key in the `[engine]` section of containers.conf to the directory containing your helper binaries.
```

In addition, the sockets within my WSL instance I use to work now show only the `podman-root.sock` and not the `podman-user.sock`. 

```
❯ echo $CONTAINER_HOST
unix:///mnt/wsl/podman-sockets/podman-machine-default/podman-user.sock
❯ podman image ls
Cannot connect to Podman. Please verify your connection to the Linux system using `podman system connection list`, or try `podman machine init` and `podman machine start` to manage a new Linux VM
Error: unable to connect to Podman socket: Get "http://d/v5.7.1/libpod/_ping": dial unix /mnt/wsl/podman-sockets/podman-machine-default/podman-user.sock: connect: no such file or directory: unix:///mnt/wsl/podman-sockets/podman-machine-default/podman-user.sock
❯ ls -al /mnt/wsl/podman-sockets/podman-machine-default
total 0
drwxrwxrwx 2 root root 60 Dec 12 12:02 .
drwxrwxrwx 3 root root 60 Dec 12 12:02 ..
srw-rw---- 1 root   10  0 Dec 12 12:02 podman-root.sock
``` 

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. `scoop update podman`
2. `podman machine start`


### Describe the results you received

VM starts but doesn't mount the sockets and shows error related to `win-sshproxy.exe`.

### Describe the results you expected

Things keep working as expected. 

### podman info output

```
PS C:\Users\ruben> podman info
OS: windows/amd64
provider: wsl
version: 5.7.1

Cannot connect to Podman. Please verify your connection to the Linux system using `podman system connection list`, or try `podman machine init` and `podman machine start` to manage a new Linux VM
Error: unable to connect to Podman socket: Get "http://d/v5.7.1/libpod/_ping": ssh: rejected: connect failed (open failed)
```

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

None

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Additional environment details

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body:
Actually the connection list on the windows host seem... not correct? 

```
PS C:\Users\ruben> podman system connection list
Name                         URI                                                          Identity                                                       Default     ReadWrite
podman-machine-default       ssh://user@127.0.0.1:60654/run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock  C:\Users\ruben\.local\share\containers\podman\machine\machine  true        true
podman-machine-default-root  ssh://root@127.0.0.1:60654/run/podman/podman.sock            C:\Users\ruben\.local\share\containers\podman\machine\machine  false       true
```

but it seems there's only the root socket, even if the VM is in rootless mode...
I've `scoop reset podman@5.7.0` and I'm not getting the errors anymore about `win-sshproxy`, but the user socket doesn't seem exported anymore, even if it's in the VM `fstab`.

Ok, I've investigated, and the `user@1000.service` unit is failing in the VM. 

```
[user@Baaj ~]$ systemctl status user@1000.service
× user@1000.service - User Manager for UID 1000
     Loaded: loaded (/usr/lib/systemd/system/user@.service; static)
    Drop-In: /usr/lib/systemd/system/user@.service.d
             └─10-login-barrier.conf
             /usr/lib/systemd/system/service.d
             └─10-timeout-abort.conf
             /etc/systemd/system/user@.service.d
             └─delegate.conf
     Active: failed (Result: resources)
 Invocation: 4c9608bc8cbe4510b3d668445f4a2799
       Docs: man:user@.service(5)
      Tasks: 2
     Memory: 5.6M (peak: 9.5M)
        CPU: 0
     CGroup: /user.slice/user-1000.slice/user@1000.service

Dec 13 23:04:55 Baaj systemd[1]: user@1000.service: Failed with result 'resources'.
Dec 13 23:04:55 Baaj systemd[1]: Failed to start user@1000.service - User Manager for UID 1000.
Dec 13 23:04:56 Baaj systemd[1]: user@1000.service: Failed to spawn executor: Device or resource busy
Dec 13 23:04:56 Baaj systemd[1]: user@1000.service: Failed to spawn 'start' task: Device or resource busy
Dec 13 23:04:56 Baaj systemd[1]: user@1000.service: Failed with result 'resources'.
Dec 13 23:04:56 Baaj systemd[1]: Failed to start user@1000.service - User Manager for UID 1000.
Dec 13 23:05:32 Baaj systemd[1]: user@1000.service: Failed to spawn executor: Device or resource busy
Dec 13 23:05:32 Baaj systemd[1]: user@1000.service: Failed to spawn 'start' task: Device or resource busy
Dec 13 23:05:32 Baaj systemd[1]: user@1000.service: Failed with result 'resources'.
Dec 13 23:05:32 Baaj systemd[1]: Failed to start user@1000.service - User Manager for UID 1000.
```

Ok, I think I got it. The UID of the user of the VM of podman and the user of my "work WSL instance" is the same (1000). And if you have systemd enabled, it collides. So the first VM which runs will get the user running, and the other one will get the "Device Busy". 

This seems a bit weird tho...

Yes. Changing my work WSL UID of the default user from `1000` to `2000` fixes my problem with running `user@1000` inside the podman VM and I got my user socket back in `/run/user/1000/podman`. . Still, this was not needed before and I believe the two VMs should not interact?

Thank you @rdbisme. Been wondering what the issue was. Changing UID fixed the problem. The default VM should not interact with other VMs. If this is a WSL specific issue then Podman VM should pick another UID by default.

What helped me was creating the containers.conf file with the following content in the folder C:\Users\<User-ID>\AppData\Roaming\containers.
```
[engine]
# Standard-Container-Laufzeit (z. B. runc, crun)
runtime = "crun"

helper_binaries_dir = ["C:\\Users\\<User-ID>\\scoop\\apps\\podman\\current"]

# Standard-Image-Registries
image_default_transport = "docker://"

# Rootless-Modus aktivieren
events_logger = "journald"

[containers]
# Standard-Umgebung fÃ¼r Container
env = ["PATH=/usr/local/bin:/usr/bin:/bin"]

# Standard-Netzwerkmodus
netns = "host"

# Standard-User fÃ¼r Container
userns = "host"

[network]
# Standard-CNI-Netzwerk
default_network = "podman"

[secrets]
# Pfad fuer Container-Secrets
driver = "file"
```

With my scoop install of 5.7.1 none of the suggestions are working for me: Windows Insider Beta 26220.7523 ge_release

I am not sure how to "change the UID of Work WSL", I did have a Debian WSL with default UID of 1000 so I just unregistered it.

* Removed all other WSL distributions
* Shut down all Hyper-V VMs
* Specified correct **helper_binaries_dir** with `containers.conf` located both in `$HOME\.config\containers` as well as `$Env:APPDATA\Roaming\containers`

Always am getting the $BINDIR error, but the `podman info` is working:

```
~\.config\containers
❯ podman machine start
Starting machine "podman-machine-default"

This machine is currently configured in rootless mode. If your containers
require root permissions (e.g. ports < 1024), or if you run into compatibility
issues with non-podman clients, you can switch using the following command:

        podman machine set --rootful

time="2026-01-01T11:17:33-07:00" level=warning msg="Failed to find $BINDIR: The system cannot find the path specified."
API forwarding for Docker API clients is not available due to the following startup failures.
        could not find "win-sshproxy.exe" in one of [$BINDIR].  To resolve this error, set the helper_binaries_dir key in the `[engine]` section of containers.conf to the directory containing your helper binaries.

Podman clients are still able to connect.
Machine "podman-machine-default" started successfully
```

Encounter a similar issue here.

I got this with podman 5.7.1:
```console
Starting machine "podman-machine-default"

This machine is currently configured in rootless mode. If your containers
require root permissions (e.g. ports < 1024), or if you run into compatibility
issues with non-podman clients, you can switch using the following command:

        podman machine set --rootful

time="2026-01-19T12:43:15-08:00" level=warning msg="Failed to find $BINDIR: The system cannot find the path specified."
API forwarding for Docker API clients is not available due to the following startup failures.
        could not find "win-sshproxy.exe" in one of [$BINDIR].  To resolve this error, set the helper_binaries_dir key in the `[engine]` section of containers.conf to the directory containing your helper binaries.

Podman clients are still able to connect.
Machine "podman-machine-default" started successfully
```
And podman-desktop is complaining about:
```console
Error while executing podman run quay.io/podman/hello: Error: connect ENOENT \.\pipe\podman-machine-default
```
However, podman-desktop can connect to the machine terminal somehow.

But, with podman 5.7.0, it all works:
```console
Starting machine "podman-machine-default"

This machine is currently configured in rootless mode. If your containers
require root permissions (e.g. ports < 1024), or if you run into compatibility
issues with non-podman clients, you can switch using the following command:

        podman machine set --rootful

API forwarding listening on: npipe:////./pipe/docker_engine

Docker API clients default to this address. You do not need to set DOCKER_HOST.
Machine "podman-machine-default" started successfully
```
And podman-desktop is happy.


OS: Windows 11 Pro
OS build: 26200.7462

I have experienced the same problem as others above.
On Windows 11 machine, latest podman (client 5.7.1, server 5.5.2), installed with scoop: `podman machine start` fails with error quoted above about `win-sshproxy.exe` not found in `$BINDIR`.

Many thanks to silver886 for his workaround: 
reverting to 5.7.0 works!

I hope this helps narrow the debugging: the breaking change is between 5.7.0 and 5.7.1.
